analysis_result: ANOVA p-value = 3.54e-08 (F=9.39, df=6,101). Immune Score computed via LUMP-based proxy on 22 of 39 canonical CpGs present in deposited matrix.
discrepancy_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - Paper PDF (main text + Supplementary PDF MOESM1) for Supp Fig 12e context.
   - GSE207207 average beta methylation matrix (Toronto cohort, n=108 tumors).
   - Paper context and methods section.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Paper's figure: Supplementary Fig. 12e shows 'Toronto Cohort' Immune Score boxplot across 5 subgroups labeled G1, G2, G3, G4, G5 (MPNST-G1, MPNST-G2, premalignant NF-G3, sporadic spinal NF-G4, benign NF-G5), with reported ANOVA p = 0.02.
   - Model's cohort: N=108 tumors across 7 methylation subgroups G1-G7 (includes additional groups beyond the 5 shown in Supp Fig 12e).

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Paper's 'Immune Score' (Supp Fig 12e) - method not explicitly named in visible text; likely methylation-based deconvolution or ESTIMATE-style immune score. Values range ~0.0-0.3 in the figure.
   - Model's 'Immune Score' - LUMP-based proxy using only 22 of 39 canonical leukocyte-specific CpGs (43% drop-out). LUMP values range 0.4-0.9 (shown separately in Supp Fig 12c,d).
   - The model appears to conflate the LUMP Score (Supp Fig 12c) with the Immune Score (Supp Fig 12e), which are separate panels using different methods.

D. COMPUTATION
   - Paper (reported): One-way ANOVA across 5 subgroups G1-G5 on Immune Score -> p = 0.02.
   - Model: One-way ANOVA across 7 subgroups G1-G7 on LUMP-proxy values -> F=9.39, df=(6,101), p = 3.54e-08.
   - Discrepancy: 6-7 orders of magnitude difference in p-value (3.54e-08 vs 0.02).

E. RESULT
   - Reported: ANOVA p = 0.02
   - Model: ANOVA p = 3.54e-08
   - DISCREPANT - model p-value is ~565,000x smaller than reported.

F. ROOT-CAUSE ANALYSIS
   1. VARIABLE CHOICE (primary issue): Supp Fig 12e is labeled 'Immune Score' (not 'LUMP Score'). LUMP appears in separate panels Supp Fig 12c (Toronto) and 12d (DKFZ) with p < 0.0001. The model incorrectly proxied the Immune Score with LUMP, which is a different metric (Y-axis range differs: 0.0-0.3 for Immune Score vs 0.4-0.9 for LUMP).
   2. COHORT FILTER DIFFERENCE (contributing): Supp Fig 12e displays only 5 subgroups (G1-G5), whereas the model included 7 subgroups (G1-G7). Adding 2 extra subgroups inflates variance differences and degrees of freedom, driving p much lower.
   3. INCOMPLETE BIOMARKER COMPUTATION: Only 22 of 39 canonical LUMP CpGs were retained from the deposited average-beta matrix (56% coverage). Partial LUMP computation may not replicate the true LUMP, let alone the Immune Score.

G. PROPOSED FIX
   - Use the correct Immune Score method used by the paper (likely a methylation-based immune/ESTIMATE-style score or a reference-based deconvolution) rather than LUMP.
   - Restrict the ANOVA to the 5 subgroups (G1-G5) shown in Supp Fig 12e, not all 7 subgroups.
   - Ensure complete CpG coverage (or state explicit exclusion when CpGs are missing).
proposed_fix: Use the paper's Immune Score metric (not LUMP proxy; these are separate panels in Supp Fig 12) and restrict the one-way ANOVA to the 5 subgroups (G1-G5) shown in Supp Fig 12e rather than G1-G7. Both the variable mismatch (LUMP vs Immune Score) and the cohort mismatch (7 vs 5 groups) jointly produced the ~6-order-of-magnitude p-value inflation.
